_instructions.how_to_export_slides: In PowerPoint → File → Export → Export to JPEG/PNG → All Slides
_instructions.pdf_support: You can also place a PDF here named 'slides.pdf' and it will be shown in an iframe

title: Welcome to The Tinkering Lab
subtitle: Introduction to STEAM & Electronics Basics
overview: This is NOT a normal class. No memorising, no textbooks, no sitting quietly. You will BUILD things, BREAK things, and LEARN from both. In this lab, mistakes are not failures — they are data.

slides._note: Export your PPT slides as images (JPG/PNG) into sessions/session-1/slides/ folder. Name them slide-01.jpg, slide-02.jpg etc. OR put your PDF as slides.pdf in this folder.
slides._pdf_note: If you have a PDF, set 'pdf' above to the path. The site will show it in an embedded viewer.

activity.title: Build Your First LED Circuit
steps: Place LED — long leg (+) to resistor end

quiz.q: What does STEAM stand for?
quiz.explanation: STEAM stands for Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics — five subjects that work together in every real-world project.

quiz.q: What is tinkering?
options: Learning by doing — trying, failing, and improving
quiz.explanation: Tinkering = Learning by DOING! Try something → See what happens → Fix what broke → Try again — better!

quiz.q: What does Voltage mean in electronics?
options: The amount of water in a pipe
The force that pushes electricity (like water pressure)
How many components are in a circuit
quiz.explanation: Voltage (V) is like water pressure — it's the force that pushes electricity. Higher voltage = stronger push!

quiz.q: What is Current in electronics?
options: How much electricity flows through the circuit (measured in Amps)
quiz.explanation: Current (I) is measured in Amps (A). Think of it as how much water flows — the amount of electricity moving through the circuit.

quiz.q: What is Resistance in electronics?
options: Opposition that slows down electrical flow (measured in Ohms)
quiz.explanation: Resistance (R) is like a narrow pipe — it slows down the flow. Resistors protect components from too much current.

quiz.q: Why do we use a resistor with an LED?
options: To protect the LED from too much current
To make the circuit more complex
To change the colour of the LED
quiz.explanation: Without a resistor, too much current flows through the LED and burns it out. The resistor limits current to a safe level.

quiz.q: Which leg of an LED is positive (+)?
quiz.explanation: The longer leg of an LED is the positive (+) anode. The shorter leg is negative (–). Polarity matters!

quiz.q: What is a breadboard used for?
quiz.explanation: A breadboard lets you build circuits temporarily by plugging in components. No soldering needed — perfect for experimenting!

quiz.q: What is Arduino?
options: A microcontroller board that can run programs to control hardware
quiz.explanation: Arduino is a microcontroller board. It's the brain of your circuit — you write programs to control LEDs, motors, sensors and more!

quiz.q: In the water analogy, what does a 'tap' represent?
quiz.explanation: In the water analogy: Tap open = switch ON → electricity flows. Tap closed = switch OFF → electricity stops.

quiz.q: What happens if you reverse an LED?
options: It doesn't light up — LEDs only work in one direction
quiz.explanation: LEDs are diodes — they only allow current to flow in ONE direction. Reverse it and no current flows.

quiz.q: What is a short circuit?
options: A circuit that is very short in length
Connecting + directly to – without any component in between
A circuit with only one LED
quiz.explanation: A short circuit happens when + connects directly to – without a component. This causes dangerous heat!

quiz.q: What should you do if you smell burning from your circuit?
options: Disconnect power immediately and tell the teacher
Try to fix it while it's still connected
quiz.explanation: Safety first! Burning smell = disconnect immediately and tell the teacher. Never ignore warning signs!

quiz.q: What does 'Fail Loudly' mean in our lab culture?
options: Make loud noises when something breaks
Share your failures so everyone can learn from them
Fail on purpose to get attention
quiz.explanation: Fail Loudly means: If something doesn't work, SHARE IT! Everyone learns from your mistake. Failures are data!

quiz.q: Which of these is a tool in the Tinkering Lab?
quiz.explanation: Your lab has 6 tools: Arduino (brain), Sensors (eyes & ears), Motors (muscles), IoT, 3D Printing, Electronics!

quiz.q: What does IoT stand for?
quiz.explanation: IoT = Internet of Things! Connecting physical devices to the internet so they can send/receive data remotely.

quiz.q: Thomas Edison failed how many times before inventing the light bulb?
options: He got it right the first time
quiz.explanation: Thomas Edison failed about 1000 times! Each failure taught him something new. That's the tinkerer's mindset!

quiz.q: What is the 'Hello World' of electronics?
quiz.explanation: Making an LED blink/glow is the 'Hello World' of electronics — the first thing every engineer builds!

quiz.q: In the roadmap, what comes after 'Foundation'?
quiz.explanation: The roadmap: 01 Foundation → 02 Sensors → 03 Motion → 04 Connectivity → Final Project!

quiz.q: What is the job of a Motor in electronics?
quiz.explanation: Motors are the 'muscles' of electronics! They convert electrical energy into movement.

quiz.q: Why should you 'Ask before connecting power'?
options: To make sure your circuit is safe and correct before powering on
Because the teacher likes being asked
quiz.explanation: Always check with your teacher before powering on — prevents short circuits and safety hazards!

quiz.q: What does 'Iterate Always' mean?
options: Give up after the first failure
Version 1 is never the best — keep improving your design
quiz.explanation: Iteration is the engineer's superpower! Version 1 is never the best. Keep testing and improving!

quiz.q: In a circuit, Resistor slows current like:
options: A pump that speeds water up
Rocks in a pipe that slow water down
A fountain that shoots water up
quiz.explanation: A resistor is like rocks in a water pipe — they slow down the flow, protecting components from too much current.

quiz.q: What is special about 3D Printing in the lab?
options: You can design custom parts and physically print them
It only prints in one colour
quiz.explanation: 3D Printing = The Maker! Design a custom part digitally and it gets physically printed in 3D!

quiz.q: What should you bring to the next session?
options: Curiosity, a question you're still thinking about, and your enthusiasm!
quiz.explanation: Come with Curiosity + A question + Your enthusiasm! The best tool is your curious mind!
